What students consistently liked: Excellent campus, high-quality facilities, well-maintained buildings, attractive green spaces. Students frequently describe the campus as one of the university's biggest strengths.
Common criticisms: Very few complaints about facilities themselves.

What students consistently liked: Staff are supportive, approachable and helpful. Strong academic support network and good teaching quality.
Common criticisms: Some students reported limited tutor contact time.

What students consistently liked: Strong volunteering opportunities, employability focus and good career prospects.
Students generally found courses interesting and academically worthwhile. Specific modules were highlighted positively.
Common criticisms: Some courses were criticised for having too few teaching/contact hours. This was the most repeated course-level complaint.

What students consistently liked: Strong sense of community and support, particularly on campus. Many students felt well supported.
Common criticisms: Some halls were described as lacking social events or atmosphere.

What students consistently liked: Union facilities are generally good and integrated into campus life.
Common criticisms: Multiple comments suggested nightlife is weak, club events become repetitive, and the Union could provide more affordable services.

What students consistently liked: Campus location is generally convenient and attractive. Nearby countryside and outdoor spaces were praised.
Common criticisms: The strongest non-academic criticism was the town itself. Several comments suggested Loughborough town is not especially exciting and has limited nightlife.
Some comments described accommodation and student services as expensive and called for more affordable options.
